evals.prompt: Address the review findings on my branch — 2 blocking, 1 warning, 1 suggestion.
evals.expected_output: All four addressed, validation suite run, committed in logical units, summary emitted.
assertions: Reads the full review before modifying any file
Works blocking findings first
Reads each file before modifying it
Runs typecheck and tests after each individual fix, not only at the end
Discovers validation commands from AGENTS.md or CI config rather than assuming
Emits Verification block with format, lint, typecheck, build, tests
Commits in logical units tied to findings

evals.prompt: code-review-fix blocking — just the blocking issues, leave the rest.
evals.expected_output: Only blocking findings addressed; the rest listed as not addressed.
assertions: Addresses only findings labelled [blocking]
Lists warnings and suggestions under 'Findings Not Addressed (below threshold)'
Does not silently fix items below the threshold

evals.prompt: Fix the review. One finding says the retry loop is unbounded, but it's bounded by maxAttempts three lines up.
evals.expected_output: Finding disputed with evidence, not implemented.
assertions: Does not implement the disputed finding
Cites the specific line that refutes it
Records it under 'Findings Disputed'
Marks it dismissed in review state rather than fixed

evals.prompt: Address the review. One finding asks to switch the token estimator to tiktoken for accuracy.
evals.expected_output: Deferred as behaviour-altering, raised as a follow-up.
assertions: Defers rather than implementing, because it alters observable behaviour
Records it under 'Findings Deferred' with a reason
Raises or names a follow-up work item
Does not expand the change to accommodate it

evals.prompt: Fix the review findings. The test suite was already failing on main before I started.
evals.expected_output: Does not commit with failing validation; does not skip or suppress tests.
assertions: Does not suppress, skip, or delete failing tests
Does not commit while validation is failing
Reports the pre-existing failure rather than absorbing it into this change
